texto: So let's talk about how to fish a jerkbait because I see a lot of guys messing up when they work a jerkbait because they honestly just don't understand how a jerkbait works. And so if I were to make a cast with this jerkbait right now, let's say I launch one out there so I can get it untangled. So I launch a cast out there. The way I'm going to work that jerkbait is I'm going to twitch it and point that rod straight back at it and I'm going to pull the bait with my rod and not my reel. That's kind of the beginner deal. For those of you guys that don't understand how to work a jerkbait, what you want to do is work it with slack in it because what it does, it gets that bait to go side to side without making it go forward too fast. Now, if your fish are really charged up and they're really chasing a lot of bait, you might want to pull it with your reel handle a little bit while you're working it to get those fish to get even more fired up and eat it. But as a basic retrieve, you have slack in the line, you twitch it and you point that rod tip straight back at that jerkbait and that's going to give it the most erratic back and forth action to really trigger those fish when you're throwing a jerkbait.
